claims.text: Recent regional-science literature provides strong accounts of left-behind places, development traps, resilience, institutions, and place-based policy, but the bid-award-procurement-contract project pipeline is less often treated as the central empirical object.

sources.title: OpenAlex Works API and regional-science corpus pull
sources.notes: Used as the corpus backbone for mapping recent open-access regional-science work and full-text links where OpenAlex exposes them.

sources.title: The Regional Development Trap in Europe
sources.authors: Andreas Diemer, Simona Iammarino, Andres Rodriguez-Pose, Michael Storper
sources.notes: Used for the development-trap framing that motivates studying mechanisms that reproduce regional underperformance.

sources.title: Reframing Urban and Regional Development for Left Behind Places
sources.authors: Danny MacKinnon, Louise Kempton, Peter O'Brien, Emma Ormerod, Andy Pike, John Tomaney
sources.notes: Used for the left-behind places literature and the need to rethink urban and regional development.

sources.title: Left Behind Places: What Are They and Why Do They Matter?
sources.authors: Ron Martin, Andy Pike, Peter Tyler, Ben Gardiner
sources.notes: Used for contemporary definitions and mechanisms around left-behind places.

sources.title: An Agenda for a Reformed Cohesion Policy
sources.notes: Used for place-based policy logic and the importance of institutional and territorial fit.

sources.title: Regional Inequality in Europe: Evidence, Theory and Policy Implications
sources.authors: Simona Iammarino, Andres Rodriguez-Pose, Michael Storper
sources.notes: Used for the regional inequality and policy-implications foundation.

uncertainties.uncertainty: OpenAlex coverage and full-text links are uneven across venues and repositories.
uncertainties.current_treatment: The draft treats OpenAlex as a corpus backbone, not an exhaustive reading substitute.
uncertainties.next_audit: Run the full OA pull, record failed full-text URLs, and compare against core-journal hand searches.

claims.text: A project pipeline can be operationalised as conversions from need to project idea, bid, award, procurement, contract, and delivery.

sources.title: Levelling Up Fund Round 2: Successful Bidders
sources.notes: Used for the existence of published place-based funding awards and project-level funding-window data.

sources.title: Contracts Finder
sources.notes: Used for the claim that lower-value public procurement opportunities and awards are searchable and usable for the procurement stage.

sources.title: Contracts Finder API Help Page
sources.notes: Used for structured procurement access through Contracts Finder endpoints.

sources.title: Open Contracting
sources.notes: Used for the claim that Contracts Finder and Find a Tender notices can be downloaded in OCDS-compatible formats.

sources.title: UK Project-Pipeline Data Manifest
sources.notes: Used for the explicit list of project-pipeline stages, source fields, and fetch status.

uncertainties.uncertainty: Procurement notices are imperfect proxies for delivery and may miss bundled or renamed projects.
uncertainties.current_treatment: The manuscript labels procurement and contract stages separately from delivery.
uncertainties.next_audit: Validate matched notices against completion reports, local accounts, and project monitoring data.

claims.text: Competitive and projectified regional policy can advantage places that already possess administrative capacity, fiscal slack, planning bandwidth, consultant access, and pre-existing project shelves.

sources.title: Reframing Urban and Regional Development for Left Behind Places
sources.authors: Danny MacKinnon, Louise Kempton, Peter O'Brien, Emma Ormerod, Andy Pike, John Tomaney
sources.notes: Used for the left-behind places literature and the need to rethink urban and regional development.

sources.title: An Agenda for a Reformed Cohesion Policy
sources.notes: Used for place-based policy logic and the importance of institutional and territorial fit.

sources.title: Regional Inequality in Europe: Evidence, Theory and Policy Implications
sources.authors: Simona Iammarino, Andres Rodriguez-Pose, Michael Storper
sources.notes: Used for the regional inequality and policy-implications foundation.

sources.title: Levelling Up Fund Round 2: Successful Bidders
sources.notes: Used for the existence of published place-based funding awards and project-level funding-window data.

sources.title: Local Authority Revenue Expenditure and Financing
sources.notes: Used as a candidate source for fiscal capacity and local authority resource proxies.

sources.title: UK Project-Pipeline Data Manifest
sources.notes: Used for the explicit list of project-pipeline stages, source fields, and fetch status.

sources.title: Levelling Up Fund Round 2 Explanatory Note on the Assessment and Decision-Making Process
sources.authors: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities
sources.notes: Used for the official Round 2 bid count, requested value, selection count, assessment process, and decision criteria.

sources.title: Levelling Up Fund Process Evaluation: Phase 1 Final Report
sources.authors: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
sources.notes: Used for reported capacity funding, bid assessment process, and Round 3 movement away from repeated competitive applications.

uncertainties.uncertainty: Local administrative capacity is not directly observed in one clean variable.
uncertainties.current_treatment: The draft proposes a bundle of fiscal, staffing, planning, and governance proxies.
uncertainties.next_audit: Build sensitivity checks across alternative capacity proxies.

uncertainties.uncertainty: Procurement notices are imperfect proxies for delivery and may miss bundled or renamed projects.
uncertainties.current_treatment: The manuscript labels procurement and contract stages separately from delivery.
uncertainties.next_audit: Validate matched notices against completion reports, local accounts, and project monitoring data.

uncertainties.uncertainty: The paper has not yet estimated local-authority conversion models beyond the aggregate bid-to-award anchor.
uncertainties.current_treatment: Estimate slots are labelled as pending for procurement, contract, and delivery stages.
uncertainties.next_audit: Build the administrative table and revise the warrant level of each empirical claim.

uncertainties.uncertainty: The Round 2 aggregate conversion rate does not identify whether high-need or low-capacity places were disadvantaged.
uncertainties.current_treatment: The descriptive table is framed as an observable pipeline anchor, not as a causal test.
uncertainties.next_audit: Join bid, award, need, and capacity records at local-authority level.

claims.text: For Levelling Up Fund Round 2, 529 submitted bids sought £8.8 billion and 111 bids worth about £2.09 billion were selected, giving an aggregate bid-to-award conversion rate of 21.0 percent by count.

sources.title: Levelling Up Fund Round 2: Successful Bidders
sources.notes: Used for the existence of published place-based funding awards and project-level funding-window data.

sources.title: Levelling Up Fund Round 2 Explanatory Note on the Assessment and Decision-Making Process
sources.authors: Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities
sources.notes: Used for the official Round 2 bid count, requested value, selection count, assessment process, and decision criteria.

sources.title: Shovel-Ready Trap Analysis Manifest and Seed Data Artifacts
sources.notes: Used for the committed generated CSV and JSON artifacts: parsed LUF Round 2 awards, stage counts, country summary, and OpenAlex corpus summary.

uncertainties.uncertainty: The Round 2 aggregate conversion rate does not identify whether high-need or low-capacity places were disadvantaged.
uncertainties.current_treatment: The descriptive table is framed as an observable pipeline anchor, not as a causal test.
uncertainties.next_audit: Join bid, award, need, and capacity records at local-authority level.
